title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. aurulentiforme
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. aurulentiforme, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion-like group of plants in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. aurulentiforme can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, or as a tincture, extracted using a solvent such as ethanol.
